pages.text: Create 6 adds a full $(bold)package logistics$() system — think parcels travelling your base on demand.$(br2)A $(bold)Packager$() boxes items from an inventory into a $(bold)package$(). Packages ride belts and chutes like any item, but they also know their $(italic)address$() and can be routed automatically.
pages.title: Packages

pages.text: $(bold)Sending and requesting:$()$(br)$(li)$(bold)Postbox / Frogport$() — send and receive packages across your network by address.$(li)$(bold)Stock Link$() + $(bold)Stock Ticker$() — register your storage to a network, then $(italic)request$() items from a list, like ordering from a catalogue.$(li)$(bold)Redstone Requester$() — auto-orders a set amount when a signal fires.
pages.title: Ordering

pages.text: This system replaces a lot of hand-built sorting: you keep a wall of vaults behind Stock Links, and pull what you need from a Stock Ticker anywhere it reaches.$(br2)Ponder's package scenes are especially worth watching — the addressing UI is easier shown than written.
pages.title: Why It Matters
